title: Time Reversal
summary: This page covers time compression and expansion of sinusoids (Drill 1.5) and introduces time reversal of signals. Time reversal of x(t) is achieved by replacing t with -t, reflecting the signal about the vertical axis, resulting in x(-t), while reflection about the horizontal axis gives -x(t).

figures.caption: Figure 1.8 Time reversal of a signal.
figures.description: The figure contains two time-domain plots that demonstrate time reversal. Panel (a) shows the original signal x(t), with a slanted segment from t=0 to t=5 and a curved segment near negative time; panel (b) shows phi(t)=x(-t), the mirror image of the same waveform about the vertical t=0 axis. Students should notice that every event originally at time t appears at time -t after reversal, while amplitudes are not negated.
